description: Minor Prophets — domain-specific warning systems. Each domain has its own failure patterns that general Canon checks won't catch.

warnings: Modifying files outside the mandate's stated scope without authorization
Adding dependencies without checking existing ones first
Writing tests that mock the thing they're testing (false confidence)
Refactoring during a feature mandate (scope creep in disguise)
Reading more than 10 files without producing findings (gluttony)
Confirmation bias — only reading sources that support the hypothesis
Presenting uncertain findings without edge case flags
Duplicating another Analyst's coverage without checking territory
Documenting aspirational behavior instead of actual behavior
Adding comments that restate code rather than explaining why
Writing docs from memory instead of reading current source
Documenting internal implementation details in user-facing docs
Projecting the user model onto customer needs
Treating survey data as behavioral data
Ignoring negative signals in favor of confirming signals
Recommending solutions before the research mandate is complete
Spawn plans with more agents than the mandate requires (Babel)
Plans that assume perfect handoff between agents
Plans that don't account for the Temptation at each stage
Optimistic manna estimates without historical basis

note: Agents should check domain-warnings.json during Genesis Phase if their mandate matches a listed domain. The warnings are not Canon rules — they are domain-specific heuristics that supplement Proverbs. New domains and warnings are added as the system gains experience.
